The bark of Erythrophleum soyauxii has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating malaria, and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The bioactive constituents of Erythrophleum soyauxii include alkaloids such as erythrophleine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the bark of Erythrophleum soyauxii is made into herbal preparations, including decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments.
